# StenoDrop

> StenoDrop is a free, open-source batch audio transcription app for macOS,
> Windows, and Linux. Users drag in audio files or whole folders; every file
> is transcribed locally (Whisper small model) and saved as a .txt transcript
> next to its source. Nothing is uploaded — it works fully offline.

Key facts:
- Price: free, no account, no usage limits, open source (github.com/fahdi/stenodrop)
- Platforms: native Swift app for macOS 14+; Tauri (Rust) app for Windows 10+ and Linux; mobile planned
- Languages: ~100 via Whisper, including first-class Urdu; optional one-toggle translation to English
- Formats: wav, mp3, m4a, flac, ogg, opus, aiff, caf, voice memos, and audio tracks of mp4/mov/webm video
- Output: <filename>.txt written beside each audio file; transcripts also viewable/copyable in the app
- Privacy: all processing on-device; the Whisper model (~466 MB) downloads once on first run
- macOS extras: live recording with rolling transcription, Metal GPU acceleration
- macOS prerequisites: brew install whisper-cpp ffmpeg; Windows/Linux builds bundle the engine

Downloads: https://github.com/fahdi/stenodrop/releases/latest
Site: https://fahdi.github.io/stenodrop/
